name: Monarch Butterfly
summary: Monarch butterflies are beautiful insects famous for their bright orange and black wings. They are best known for their incredible long-distance migration across North America and their unique relationship with milkweed plants, which is the only food their caterpillars eat.
metaTitle: Monarch Butterfly Facts | Habitat, Diet, Migration & Photos
metaDescription: Discover monarch butterfly facts with photos, caterpillar life cycle, long-distance migration, milkweed diet, and conservation. Learn how they grow and migrate.

lifespan.wild: 2 to 6 weeks (summer generations); up to 8 months (migrating generation)
lifespan.captivity: Up to 8 months in climate-controlled sanctuaries

funFacts: Monarch caterpillars eat only milkweed leaves, which contain a toxin that makes them taste terrible to birds.
A migrating monarch butterfly can fly up to 80 miles (130 km) in a single day.
Monarchs are the only butterflies that make a two-way migration, similar to birds.
A group of monarch butterflies is called a flutter, a swarm, or a kaleidoscope!
Inside the chrysalis, the caterpillar's body completely breaks down before reforming into a butterfly.
Monarch butterflies use the sun as a compass to guide them on their long journey south.

faq.question: What is a monarch butterfly?
faq.answer: A monarch butterfly is a large, colorful insect famous for its bright orange wings with black stripes and white spots. They are known for traveling thousands of miles every year.

faq.question: Why do monarchs eat milkweed?
faq.answer: Milkweed is the only food that monarch caterpillars eat. The plant has a special sap that makes the caterpillar and the adult butterfly taste bad to predators like birds.

faq.question: Where do monarch butterflies go in winter?
faq.answer: In winter, millions of monarchs fly south to warm places. Monarchs from eastern North America travel to pine forests in Mexico, while western monarchs fly to eucalyptus trees in California.

faq.question: How long does a monarch butterfly live?
faq.answer: Most summer monarchs live for only two to six weeks. However, the generation that migrates south in the fall can live up to eight months to survive the winter.

faq.question: What is a baby monarch called?
faq.answer: A baby monarch is called a caterpillar or a larva. It hatches from a tiny egg, eats lots of milkweed, and later turns into a chrysalis to grow into a butterfly.

faq.question: Are monarch butterflies endangered?
faq.answer: Yes, monarch butterflies face threats from habitat loss, weather changes, and pesticides. Many communities plant milkweed to help save them.
